MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-18-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, MAR-18-2026 HEARING ON MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS MODIFIED TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED AND INSTEAD THE COURT ADOPTS THE PROPOSAL OF PLAINTIFFS OF "A PARTIAL STAY, WHEREIN DISCOVERY AND MOTION PRACTICE MAY PROCEED BUT THE CASE WILL NOT PROCEED TO THE SUMMARY MOTION PHASE OR TRIAL UNTIL THE PENDING FEDERAL APPEAL ... IS COMPLETE. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING, AS MODIFIED AT THE HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. COURT REPORTER: SHEILA PHAM; CSR: 13293; T: 415.274.9977; E: E: CALENDAR-LA@VERITEXT.COM. (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Single Assignment

Notice Of Plaintiffs William Villarroel, Liese L. Sand And Robert F. Sand?S Omnibus Motion To Lift 28 U.S.C Section 1446 Stay In First-Filed Action For Limited Purpose Of Considering This Motion To Fully Consolidate Both Actions Under The First-Filed Case Number, Extend The Partial Stay Entered In Case No. Cgc-25-631805 To Fully Consolidated Case, And Permanently Lift 28 U.S.C Section 1446 Stay In Consolidated Case

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2026 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY, RECOLOGY, INC., and RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO, SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Glen Turner, Esq., of Ongaro PC, (415) 433-3900, for Plaintiffs.
Robert W. May, Esq., of Morrison & Foerster LLP, (415) 268-7000, for Defendants.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its modified tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion to stay proceedings is denied and instead the court adopts the proposal of plaintiffs of "a partial stay, wherein discovery and motion practice may proceed but the case will not proceed to the summary motion phase or trial until the pending Federal appeal ... is complete." Defendants have not shown that permitting either discovery or motion practice to proceed would result in any unfairness, inefficiency or prejudice. Because a prior version of this case was filed five years ago, in the absence of any such unfairness, inefficiency or prejudice, it is eminently reasonable for discovery and motion practice to proceed forthwith. Furthermore, the court orders that the following language be added to the proposed order, as stated and agreed to in court: if and when the 9th District Court allows any claims to proceed in the Northern District, the parties are to meet and confer to the maximum extent possible, to ensure efficiency, judicial economy, and eliminate the risk of inconsistent rulings.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, as modified at the hearing.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Sheila Pham; CSR: 13293; T: 415.274.9977; E: sheilapham28@yahoo.com. (301/HEK)
